item.title: Awesome rewrite!
item.content:encoded:
I remembered reading this article when it first came out, and while I still got the base concept and ideas, the process to go through it all was a little murky, and the camp didn't quite feel like a "camp" to me

But WOW! The edits to the first page of the scip read like a camp pamphlet while also hinting at other details throughout the rest of the story. I liked this before, but I like it even more now! Awesome job, bruh.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: after reading through the comments, i think i mostly understand this scip, but i'm still confused as to how the parody articles caused those specific things to happen at Makarios High School.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I was originally going to describe my experience reading through this as a "nightmare", but that doesn't quite convey how boring this felt to read. It's dominated by long, extraneous asides about petty high school drama from the perspective of someone who desperately needs to Log Off, and while I get that that's the point, it makes for an incredibly unenjoyable reading experience.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Oh shucks, I can’t believe I forgot about that.

Thanks a million! It’s fixed now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Hey just wanted to bring this up. Since your article is now 5121, the links to the next offset don’t work anymore.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thanks for reading and for your critique!

Definitely a biased take here, but I don't think the article would work in such a condensed form. The length is meant to explore the multifaceted nature of such an organisation's social impact - but it's not for everyone.

That aside, you make a good point about the prose. I'll do another pass later today and tone down the parts where it's too florid without reason. If there are sections you feel are especially problematic, I'd be happy to hear it!

P.S. Happy not-quite-birthday :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I seem to be in the minority, which is fine… but I found this a bit of a slog, and the prose was so thickly purple that at times it made it difficult for me to keep going. I feel like the same story could have been told just as well or better, in a much more concise way. I don't mind format screws or super long articles— i enjoy them, in fact; this one just didn't work for me. I found myself sighing rather than feeling excited when there was a link to yet another offset. it's a novote for me.

on the upside, march 21st is my birthday, so that's neat. :D

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: After receiving feedback from Weryllium and Gentleman Thief, I've added a short preface/other bits and pieces throughout, in order to hopefully make it easier to figure out what's happening right from the start.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks for that, I’ll see how I might be able to make that work!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Response to spoilers
– hide block

Okay, the codeword thing makes sense; I recognized what those things were stand-ins for, I just didn't realize that the obfuscation was happening in-universe. I think the whole thing would work better if that fact were more obvious. I spent the whole article trying to discern exactly how and when Silas's timeline diverged from ours, and like I said, that just distracts from the real story.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Hi there, thanks for the crit.

So in a nutshell, the piece should have plenty of clues as to the ontological nature of the "bizarro reality", and the identity of the Great Benefactor / Wayfarers / Celestium etc. And I do think that it explains the goals of this organisation, its relationship with the "Bastion", and why Silas is so angry.

Once the reader gets that, I think a lot of the finer details become less tangential. But I do realise that if people aren't getting the nature of the world in the first place, it’s moot.

+ Spoilers
- Spoilers
  • The bizarro reality is our world.
  • Paul and Silas are evangelical Christians, and also SCP fans.
  • The SS is a Christian extracurricular taken to an unhealthy extreme.
  • The Bastion is the nation in which they live.
  • When Silas vents his anger on the wiki, he’s using codewords.

Let me know if you think it needs to be more obvious / if there are other potential snags - thanks!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The thing that's confusing me is the bizarro reality that Silas and Paul appear to live in. It's clearly not our world, but we only ever catch fleeting glimpses of it from a frustratingly narrow perspective. Snippets like "rampant existential clamour among civil servants, in light of dire new research about the juvenilisation of the Bastion’s limited human resources" hint at a looming darkness, but it's waaaaay in the background. And the thing is, this is clearly not the real narrative you want to tell here, so these details only serve to distract the reader. You're trying to tell this story about an angry teenager letting off steam, but I'm focused on putting together what the deal is with this "Great Benefactor" and whether or not Silas's ukulele has anything to do with Dr. Clef.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Sure and I had no idea what a darned word of this meant, but that didn't mean I wasn't enjoyin' meself. :') I feel Weryllium and I had the same experience, and this was rather something to behold, whatever it was.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thanks for reading! Glad you liked it.

Now that is a broad question, but when/if this gains more traction (that’s a big if) I might do some commentary on that. In the meantime, lmk if there’s specific things you want me to address!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+ Spoilers
- Spoilers

Paul and Silas are students who enrolled into the SS together in 2012. 4 years later, a disillusioned and hurt Silas posts one SCP spoof to the wiki each midnight, as an iconoclastic anger-venting strategy. These spoofs comprise his (and Paul’s) SS-related creative output over the years, and thus divulge his entire backstory.

Paul finds out that Silas is airing dirty laundry on the Internet, and intercepts him on the fifth day by hogging the SCP-5000 slot, urging restraint and forgiveness.

Meanwhile, in the Foundationverse, the Makarios High School undergoes events that are analogous - but not exact - to Silas’ experiences and thematic concerns. Now normally, when an author posts to the mainsite, their anomaly is realised in the Foundation’s reality (as per the SAS proposal). But Silas’ authorial intent is not to populate the SCP wiki with new anomalies, it’s to let off steam. And this steam is realised as - or “transmuted” into - a rampaging, socially-twisted Keter. A Keter that the Foundation can’t catalogue properly, because its containment files - ordained by whatever is in the Database - are Silas’ spoofs.

Paul’s intervention on Friday succeeds in consoling Silas and, in the Foundation’s reality, halting the perverse charity drive through the eagle’s sacrifice. When this fifth and final spoof gets voted off the wiki (SCP-3309), all firsthand data about, and physical evidence of, the anomaly vanishes. In real life, Silas cheers up, and gets a special gift.

I didn’t write the WD part with the intent to require prior reading, but YMMV. Thanks for highlighting that.

Do let me know if there’s anything I can do to make it more obvious (if it needs to be)!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I… have absolutely no idea what's going on. And normally I consider that a bad thing, but I really want to know more about what the hell is with this bizarro world run entirely by a distorted fusion of the Foundation and AWANA.

The pataphysical thing is tripping me up, because I can't seem to piece together what happened and why the bits of the other side are still in the format in weird places. And also I don't know anything about Watership Down except that it's apparently dark, so there's probably another dimension that's completely lost on me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This was strange and delightful. I'd love to know more about your sources and symbolism.

item.title: Re: +1
item.content:encoded: Fixed and thank you! Glad you liked it :)

Wonderful! I thought it would be a bit underwhelming going into the last part, but nope! Kind of a predictable twist, but satisfying nonetheless! +1 from me.

+ Small mistake I noticed...
- hide

Also, not sure if you can edit it now that posting is closed, but in the 4997 section, slide 3, the link leading to the "How to Write an SCP" page is broken. The link starts with "http" instead of "http:." You are missing the colon before the //. Edit: Fixed now :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Nope, 'twas the work of a troll. Was obviously way too tired to spot that debacle waiting to happen, oops.

Replaced it with blackboxes for the time being - thanks for the spot.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
um, the TinyURL link leads to a picture of a penis. I really hope that wasn't intentional.

Edit: This was resolved almost immediately, and the author has asked me to point this out.

item.title: Author Post
item.content:encoded:
“A great Shadow has departed," said Gandalf, and then he laughed and the sound was like music, or like water in a parched land; and as he listened the thought came to Sam that he had not heard laughter, the pure sound of merriment, for days upon days without count.”

J.R.R. Tolkien, The Return of the King

This is an SCP about religion, but not in the way(s) you might expect.

This is an SCP about childlike faith, and the struggle to retain it amid a vortex of depravity. It is also about what happens when you have serious, proximal encounters with that depravity in the communities you trust, and then in yourself.

Whoever you are, and whatever you believe, I hope this feeds you one way or the other.

Inspired by: Bunyan's The Pilgrim's Progress, Marilynne Robinson's Gilead, Lewis' The Great Divorce, and various real-life experiences.

+ Image credits
- Image credits

4996
Maltagliati - https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Maltagliati_bulk.jpg

4997
Modifications made in Google Slides.

Slide 2 -https://snappygoat.com/b/544969c79caf2c2b7dec244c3aaaa17611ea25b6

Slide 3 -https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:WonderCon_2015_-_Phoenix_Wright_(17049616945).jpg

https://snappygoat.com/b/14fb3b96e276287be3adc9b958261c6e790eb6b5

Map - From OpenStreetMap, © OpenStreetMap contributors
Red cross - https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Round_cross.svg

Slide 12 -
https://snappygoat.com/b/4c9cb7af758581a50fefbee627e704bc4be7bcb2

5000
Titan arum - https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/5/5c/Amorphophallus_titanum_%28corpse_flower%29_-_2.jpg

All other images were taken by me.
